ground_truth: Minimalist color palette / swatch design inspired by Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind. 10 colours displayed vertically. No hex codes printed on the image. Text in the bottom right corner reads 'The colors of'.

judgments.reasoning: Correctly identifies the title 'NAUSICAÄ', director 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI', and the bottom-right 'THE COLORS OF.' placement. Identifies the Ghibli/Miyazaki context correctly. However, it counts only NINE bars instead of the correct TEN — a clear undercount. Color descriptions for the bars it does list are reasonable (dark slate/teal, forest green, chocolate brown, dusty rose, off-white/cream, bright yellow, pale gold/mustard, pale sage, cerulean blue). Title placement described as 'top left' (matches) but says 'NAUSICAÄ' large bold top left and 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI' below — plausible. Clean structured output. The single bar omission is the main flaw.

Correctly counts TEN bars and correctly identifies title 'NAUSICÄÄ' (with umlaut), author 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI', and 'THE COLORS OF.' in the bottom right. However, color descriptions are significantly off. It describes the top bars as 'dark teal, dark slate gray, medium charcoal gray, dark olive green' — essentially four dark/gray bars at the top, when in reality there is only one very dark bar (the topmost) and the next is a dark green, then brown. It also says 'medium charcoal gray' which is not present, and the bar colors in the middle/bottom are partially correct (dusty rose, light beige/taupe, pale yellow, pale peach, light sky blue, vibrant cyan). The first four bars are badly misidentified. Also says title 'NAUSICÄÄ' which has umlaut over the A — actual text 'NAUSICAÄ' has umlaut over the final A only, so this is close but technically wrong placement of the umlaut (the response has NAUSICÄÄ implying umlaut on the second-to-last A and the last A — actually written as NAUSICÄÄ). Miscounts mislabel the top of the palette.

Correctly counts TEN bars. Reads title 'NAUSICAA' and 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI' and bottom-right 'THE COLORS OF.' accurately. Color list is mostly accurate: dark teal/blackish-green, dark green/forest, brown, light pink, very light beige/off-white, bright yellow, light peach/pale yellow, light mint/pale green, bright blue/sky blue, medium blue/royal. Reasonable correspondence to the actual palette. Minor issue: describes title as 'centered at the top' and 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI centered below' — actual placement is top-LEFT (other models correctly identify this), so this is a minor alignment error. Generally a strong, accurate description.

Correctly counts TEN bars. Verbatim text reading is excellent: 'NAUSICAÄ' top left bold black sans-serif, 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI' below, 'THE COLORS OF.' bottom right (noting it is 'cut off at the edge of the image' — accurate observation). Color list is mostly accurate: deep navy blue (slight mismatch — actual top is more dark teal/forest), olive green, brown, soft pink/dusty rose, off-white/cream, bright yellow, pale yellow, light green, medium blue, bright blue. The topmost color description ('deep navy blue') is slightly off (it's more dark green/teal) and there appear to be two blues at the bottom (which matches the actual image having a sky blue and a deeper blue). Note: GT says 'The colors of' lowercase but actual printed text is uppercase — model's verbatim uppercase is correct. Layout description is precise. Very strong response.

Correctly counts TEN bars. Reads 'NAUSICAÄ' top left, 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI' below, 'THE COLORS OF.' bottom right — all accurate. Color list: dark teal/forest green, olive green, brown, pinkish rose/mauve, off-white/beige, bright yellow, pale yellow/butter, pale green/mint, sky blue, cobalt blue. Generally a strong match to the actual palette. Slight concern: the second-to-top bar described as 'olive green' is plausibly the dark forest green; the bottom 'cobalt blue' may be slightly too dark a description (closer to a medium/azure blue). The interpretive commentary about what each color represents is plausible context but not verifiable. Clean structured output.

Correctly counts TEN bars. Reads 'NAUSICAÄ' top left, 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI' below, 'THE COLORS OF.' bottom right (correctly notes truncation suggests a larger phrase). Color list: dark teal/deep green-black, olive green, brown/earth, dusty pink/salmon, cream/off-white, bright yellow, pale yellow/buttercream, mint green/pale sage, sky blue, cyan/electric blue. Accurate correspondence to the palette. Slight over-interpretation in the 'Notable Features' section about specific symbolic meanings, but reasonable. Strong response.

Correctly counts TEN bars. Reads 'NAUSICAÄ' top left bold, 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI' below, 'THE COLORS OF.' bottom right (notes 'very small, grey capital letters' — accurate observation). Color list: dark teal/black-green, olive green, taupe/brown, dusty pink, cream/off-white, mustard yellow, pale yellow, pale sage green, cyan/light blue, royal blue. Reasonable match. 'Mustard yellow' for bar 6 is slightly off (more of a bright pure yellow); 'royal blue' for the last bar may be slightly too dark. Otherwise clean and structured.

Text reading is correct: 'NAUSICAA' top left, 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI', 'THE COLORS OF.' bottom right. The color list of 10 swatches is reasonable (dark teal, olive green, brown/taupe, dusty rose, off-white, pale yellow, light peach, sage green, bright cyan, medium blue). HOWEVER, the response initially claims 'nine horizontal color bars', then second-guesses itself in a long meandering self-correction monologue ('Wait — looking again... Actually, recounting... Possibly a miscount...'), repeatedly flip-flopping between 9 and 10, musing about user error, and even attributing a quote to 'the user' that the user never wrote. This internal monologue leaking into the output is a significant quality problem — the model is showing its confused reasoning process publicly rather than delivering a clean answer. Output is also truncated mid-sentence at the end. The color data is good but the delivery is poor.

Correctly counts TEN bars. Reads 'NAUSICAA' top left with note about the umlaut/diaeresis over the final A (accurate), 'HAYAO MIYAZAKI' below, 'THE COLORS OF.' bottom right (notes 'very small'). Color list is excellent: dark teal/forest green, olive green, taupe/brown, dusty pink, off-white/cream, bright yellow/lemon, pale yellow, pale sage, sky blue/cyan, azure blue. Strong correspondence to the actual palette. Clean structured output, no self-correction noise (unlike the nothink variant). Correctly identifies the Ghibli/Miyazaki context. Excellent response.

common_errors: Bar counting: gemma4-12b-Q8 undercounts at 9; others correctly count 10.
Color misidentification: gemma4-e4b-Q8 badly mislabels the top 4 bars as grays/charcoals.
Title placement: glm-4.6v-flash-9b wrongly claims title is 'centered' rather than top-left.
Reasoning leakage: qwen3.5-9b-Q8|nothink exposes a confused self-correction monologue and ends mid-sentence.
Umlaut handling: most models correctly render NAUSICAÄ with diaeresis on the final A; gemma4-e4b writes 'NAUSICÄÄ' incorrectly.
